topic.what: garbled-circuit-based protocol for privacy-preserving scanpath comparison supporting MultiMatch, ScanMatch, and SubsMatch algorithms
topic.how: two-party secure computation using garbled circuits with server-assisted extension enabling encrypted storage and offline data owners; XOR masking splits decryption keys reconstructed inside GC
topic.why_matters: enables cross-institution gaze behavior analysis without sharing raw eye-tracking data; supports GDPR compliance and privacy-by-design for sensitive biometric data

characteristics.what_it_measures: accuracy match between secure and plaintext scanpath comparison results
characteristics.context: ScanMatch: 0 MAE; SubsMatch: ~10^-4; MultiMatch: 0.007-0.015 across datasets

characteristics.what_it_measures: end-to-end runtime for two-party scanpath comparison on LAN

characteristics.what_itasures: data transferred between parties during secure computation

characteristics.what_it_measures: computation time under wide-area Internet conditions (100 Mbit/s, 100ms RTT)
characteristics.context: WAN2 significantly impacts MultiMatch (up to 1315s); ScanMatch: 1.9-62.8s; SubsMatch: 101.8s

characteristics.what_it_measures: local encryption time per scanpath for server-assisted protocol
characteristics.context: AES-CTR encryption with HMAC; minimal uploader burden

characteristics.what_it_measures: garbled circuit runtime for HMAC verification plus AES-CTR decryption
runtime improvement over prior homomorphic encryption approach for Needleman-Wunsch

requires: LAN or fast WAN for large MultiMatch

novelty: garbled circuit implementation of MultiMatch, ScanMatch, SubsMatch
server-assisted protocol with offline data owners and encrypted storage
key wrapping via X25519-HKDF-AES-GCM for multi-client authorization
in-circuit HMAC integrity verification and AES-CTR decryption
open_problems: malicious model extension for stronger guarantees
relaxing non-collusion assumption via split-key architecture
circuit optimization for long scanpath handling
extension to other behavioral biometrics beyond eye tracking
